content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statement of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662          322-5660              322-5901            322-6441            322-6444

March 17, 1983


Clifton E. Reed
Attorney at Law
357 Third Avenue
Third Avenue at Madrona
Chula Vista, CA  92010

                                                Re: Your Request For Advice, Our
                                                    Advice No. A-83-070

Dear Mr. Reed:

     You have requested advice as to whether members of the Chula
Vista City Council are covered by Government Code Section
84308-L/ when they act in their capacity as redevelopment
agency members.  Our advice is that they are not covered, either
in their capacity as city councilmembers or in their capacity as
members of the redevelopment board.

     Section 84308(d) provides that "legislative bodies such as
city councils ... while acting as a body or a committee thereof
are exempted from the coverage of this Section."  Thus it is
clear that the city council, acting as the city council, is
exempt from the coverage of Section 84308.

     The Council is also exempt whenever it acts "as a body or
committee thereof."  2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18438.1(b)
provides that:

          A legislative body is "acting as a body or a
     committee thereof" when:

          (1)  It acts in its entirety as itself or as the
     ex officio governing body of any other district or
     entity....


_________________

1/  All statutory references made are to the Government
Code unless otherwise indicated.

Clifton E. Reed
March 17, 1983
Page Two


     When the Chula Vista City Council designated itself as the
redevelopment agency pursuant to Health and Safety Code Section
33200, it became the ex officio governing board of the agency.
Therefore, when the City Council acts as the redevelopment
agency, it is still exempt from the coverage of Section 84308.

     You have pointed out that, as the redevelopment agency, the
Council does not act in a legislative capacity. However, the
exemption of Section 84308(d) does not depend on the type of
activity engaged in by the body in specific instances. For
example, the city council is exempt even when acting in a quasi-
judicial capacity. It is also exempt when it acts in an
administrative capacity as the Chula Vista redevelopment Board.

     If you have any further questions relating to Section 84308,
I will be happy to assist you.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        Barbara A. Milman
                                        /Chief, Legal Division

BAM:km

. .

FPFC

MAR 14 9 34 AH '83

CLIFTON E. REED
ATTORNEY AT LAW
357 Third Avenue
Third Avenue at Madrona
Chula Vista, California 92010
(619) 426-4362

March 11, 1983

Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 "K" Street
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: Request For Opinion On Behalf of the City of
Chula Vista

Gentlemen:

AB 1040 [Levine] added Section 84308 to the Government
Code. Section 84308 prohibits certain public officials from
accepting, soliciting or directing contributions of $250.00
or more from any person or his agent who has an application
for a license, permit or other entitlement pending before any
body of which the official is a member.

Subsection (d) of Section 84308 specifically excludes
legislative bodies such as city councils from the coverage of
Section 84308.

Within California there are many cities, including the
City of Chula Vista, where the City Council acts as a redevelopment agency pursuant to the Health and Safety Code Section 33200.
Redevelopment agencies routinely grant applicants permits and
other entitlements such as the right to develop property. In
Andrews v. City of San Bernardino (1959) 175 Cal. App. 2d 459,
462, the Court of Appeal held that when a city council acts as
a redevelopment agency, it acts as an administrative body not
a legislative body.

Fair Political Practices Commission
March 11, 1983                                                    Page Two




          Are city council members who act as redevelopment
agency members subject to the provisions of Section 84308?

                                        Sincerely yours,


                                        [signature]

                                        CLIFTON E. REED
                                        Special Counsel
                                        Chula Vista Redevelopment Agency


CER:jas
cc:   Chairman and Members
      Chula Vista Redevelopment Agency

      George D. Lindberg
      City/Agency Attorney

      Carlyn Galway
      League of California Cities

      David R. Chapman
      San Diego County City Attorneys' Assn.

State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5660 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441 322-6444

March 22, 1983


Clifton E. Reed
Attorney at Law
357 Third Avenue
Chula Vista, CA 92101

Re: A-83-070

Dear Mr. Reed:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political
Reform Act has been referred to me. If you have any
questions about your advice request, please contact me
directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly.
Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex
legal questions, or unless more information is needed to
answer your request, you should expect a response within 21
working days.

Very truly yours,

Barbara A. Milman
Chief
Legal Division

BAM:km
Enclosure

sections.question: Are city council members who act as redevelopment agency members subject to the provisions of Section 84308?
sections.conclusion: Our advice is that they are not covered, either in their capacity as city councilmembers or in their capacity as members of the redevelopment board.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether city council members in Chula Vista are subject to Government Code Section 84308 (which restricts contributions from applicants with pending permits) when they act in their capacity as members of a redevelopment agency.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: City council members are not covered by Section 84308 in either capacity because Section 84308(d) exempts legislative bodies and their committees, and the City Council acting as the redevelopment agency remains an exempt legislative body acting as the ex officio governing board of the agency.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Both incoming request and FPPC response included in document. Question section clearly marked in incoming request. Conclusion clearly marked in FPPC response dated March 17, 1983. Document well-structured and complete.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Are city council members who act as redevelopment agency members subject to the provisions of Section 84308?

CONCLUSION: Our advice is that they are not covered, either in their capacity as city councilmembers or in their capacity as members of the redevelopment board.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises that Chula Vista City Council members are exempt from Section 84308 restrictions when acting as redevelopment agency members because they remain a legislative body exempt from coverage even when acting in an administrative capacity.
